_comment: Illustrative LLM output. Actual themes and phrasing will vary.

went_well_themes.key_insight: Earlier design reviews, shorter standups, and pair programming all landed this sprint — the team is adopting structured work habits successfully.
On-time, on-budget delivery of the onboarding flow is a strong signal that scoping discipline is improving.
Positive Slack communication trend. Maintain async-first culture.

improvement_themes.root_cause: Estimates don't account for unknowns, rework from unclear requirements, and production interruptions. All three occurred this sprint.
No protected engineering time policy. Meetings are scheduled without regard to deep work blocks.
QA starts too late. Testing is treated as a final step rather than a parallel activity.

prioritised_actions.action: PM to write acceptance criteria before sprint planning for all tickets
QA to start testing from day 5 of 10-day sprint — adjust sprint ceremonies accordingly
Block 2-hour daily focus time for engineers — no meetings 10:00-12:00
Add 10% buffer to all estimates and review buffer usage in next retro

analysis.team_health_rationale: The ratio of went-well items to improvement items is balanced, delivered on-time, and the actions are concrete and owned. Recurring issues exist but are identified — that's a healthy team signal.
patterns_to_watch: Estimation accuracy has appeared in 2+ consecutive sprints — if it persists after buffer addition, investigate requirements quality upstream
QA bottleneck recurring — consider whether sprint length is appropriate
Focus time erosion will worsen as team grows unless policy is formalised
